title: The Eighth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: July 23, 2023

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon was preached on the Eighth Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time (also called the Season after Pentecost). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would have been 2 Kings 4:42-44 (Elisha multiplying loaves), Psalm 145:10-18, Ephesians 3:14-21, and John 6:1-21 (Jesus walking on water and the feeding of the five thousand). This places the congregation in the middle of John's "Bread of Life" discourse section, a central theological passage about Jesus as sustenance and abundance.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The 80th General Convention of the Episcopal Church concluded on July 15, 2023, just eight days before this sermon. Major decisions included approval of a new marriage canon permitting same-sex marriage, a resolution affirming the use of inclusive language for God in worship, and election of Sean Rowe as Presiding Bishop. The Diocese of Colorado would have been processing these significant national decisions within their own congregations during this exact week.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical hot, dry early summer weather with temperatures in the low to mid-90s Fahrenheit. The week of July 17-23 saw no major reported crises, but the city remained focused on drought concerns and wildfire preparedness—Colorado was entering its critical fire season. Locally, discussions around housing affordability and homeless encampments in downtown Denver's Capitol Hill area (where the Cathedral is located) were ongoing community issues.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The week of July 17-23 was dominated by former President Trump's arraignment in Miami (July 13 had been his court appearance on classified documents charges); his 2024 campaign was in full swing despite legal challenges. The debt ceiling deal signed in June was creating ongoing political reverberations. NFL training camps were beginning, and the entertainment world was tracking the Barbie movie's imminent August release and strong pre-release buzz. Notably absent was any major Supreme Court news, as the court was in recess after its final decisions in late June.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Ukraine's counteroffensive against Russian forces continued with mixed tactical results; the war had entered its 17th month with no apparent resolution. The Turkey-Syria earthquake recovery was ongoing, with international aid efforts continuing in both countries. The United Nations was preparing for climate negotiations expected later in the summer and fall, with global attention on emissions and climate commitments.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The nation was experiencing a moment of relative cultural calm before what would become a turbulent fall, though political polarization remained high. There was significant church discourse specifically about LGBTQ+ inclusion following the General Convention votes just concluded. Summer routines dominated for most Americans—vacation season was in full swing, major fall cultural events (TV premieres, film releases) were still weeks away. In Colorado, outdoor recreation and concerns about water scarcity for the dry season ahead were part of the ambient awareness.
